title: taurine
main_content: mTOR signalling has a key role downstream of taurine in leukaemia cells.
context: Amino acid availability can be sensed by RAG GTPases to facilitate mTORC1 localization to the outer lysosomal membrane40 and its interaction with RHEB GTPases41, thereby promoting mTORC1 activation.
outcome: We hypothesized that taurine may activate mTOR by promoting its interaction with RAG GTPases.
predicted_context: The mechanistic target of rapamycin (mTOR) complex 1 is a well-established cellular nutrient sensor. Upon activation by amino acids and metabolic cues, mTOR signaling promotes tumorigenesis by driving metabolic reprogramming, specifically upregulating glycolysis and biosynthetic pathways required for rapid cancer cell proliferation and survival.
predicted_outcome: Therefore, taurine supplied by the tumor microenvironment acts as a critical nutrient signal that activates the mTOR pathway in leukemic cells, thereby mechanistically driving the elevated glycolysis and metabolic reprogramming necessary to promote leukaemogenesis and support leukemic stem cell expansion.
